channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: 95% Indigenous, 3x Cheaper: India’s Divyastra Mk1 & MkII Set to Transform Loitering Munition Segment
item.description: India’s push toward self-reliance in advanced unmanned strike systems is gaining momentum, with Lucknow-based startup Hoverit outlining an ambitious roadmap for its Divyastra series of loitering munitions. The company has confirmed that both the Divyastra Mk1 and the longer-range Divyastra MkII are being developed with a target of at least 95 percent indigenous content at production stage—a significant leap in localization within a segment that has traditionally relied heavily on imports.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Lockheed Martin Signals More MH-60R Seahawk Orders for Indian Navy, Leaves Decision to Governments
item.description: Lockheed Martin has indicated that additional MH-60R Seahawk helicopters would further strengthen the Indian Navy’s operational capabilities, even as it refrained from confirming any ongoing procurement discussions or follow-on orders. The response underscores both the platform’s growing importance in India’s naval aviation ecosystem and the procedural nature of such acquisitions under government-to-government frameworks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Air Force to Manage F-404 Engines, Set Up Indigenous MRO Hub with GE
item.description: Parallel to the advancing F414 co-production agreement, GE Aerospace has secured a key contract with the Indian Air Force to establish a depot-level maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) facility in India for the F404 engines that power the Tejas Mk1A fleet. The facility will be owned and operated by the IAF, marking a critical step toward building sovereign sustainment capabilities for India’s indigenous fighter programme.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Fast-Tracks Two MRSAM Regiments as Air Defence Becomes Top Priority
item.description: India has taken another significant step toward strengthening its layered air defence network, with the Defence Procurement Board (DPB) clearing the acquisition of two regiments of the Medium Range Surface-to-Air Missile (MRSAM) system. The decision reflects a growing emphasis on robust air defence capabilities, particularly in light of recent global conflicts that have underscored the centrality of missile and drone defence in modern warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Next-Gen Air Power: The Case for a Kaveri-Powered AI VTOL Fighter Like X-BAT
item.description: India’s evolving airpower doctrine is increasingly being shaped by the need for survivability, flexibility, and rapid deployment in highly contested environments. In this context, the development of an indigenous AI-piloted Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) fighter platform powered by a Kaveri-class afterburning engine represents a logical and potentially transformative step for the Indian Air Force (IAF).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BrahMos Upgrade: India’s 800 km Range Missile Expands Strike Capability into Tibet, Xinjiang
item.description: The Indian Army is set to significantly enhance its long-range precision strike capability with the induction of the extended-range 800 km variant of the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile. This upgrade nearly doubles the reach of the land-attack version currently in service (over 450 km), giving Indian forces the ability to hold deep targets in contested regions without exposing launch platforms to immediate risk. Defence officials have confirmed that the Army is preparing a major order for this advanced variant, with clearance expected at the highest levels in the Ministry of Defence.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: How a Routine Aniketra VTOL Test Revealed Human Backbone of India’s UAV Ecosystem
item.description: In the evolving landscape of India’s defense modernization, much of the attention is directed toward platforms, payloads, and performance metrics. Yet, occasionally, an incident emerges that underscores a far more fundamental truth—that behind every technological advancement lies a human network capable of adapting, responding, and overcoming in real time. A recent field episode involving SpaceGen Aviation’s Aniketra UAV serves as a compelling case study of this dynamic interplay between innovation and operational resilience.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Vice Admiral Witnesses Abhimanyu CCA Capable of Teaming with MiG-29K Fighters
item.description: Vice Admiral Sanjay Vatsayan, AVSM, NM, Vice Chief of Naval Staff (VCNS) and his team to our Centre of Excellence at NewSpace. From the inception, NewSpace had a steady focus on R&D, to ensure that technological foundations were unshakeable before scaling to the complex systems we see today. This “fundamentals-first” approach is what allows us to transition seamlessly from lab-scale breakthroughs to mission-ready platforms.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pan-India ISI Spy Network Exposed, Fed Critical Data for Planned Strikes
item.description: The ISI-backed espionage module, which the Ghaziabad Police first busted, had surveyed sensitive locations such as naval facilities and airport zones in Mumbai. They were in the process of installing solar-powered CCTV cameras at these locations and were planning to send the live feed to their handlers in Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Al-Qaeda-Linked Base Movement Resurfaces, Agencies Step Up Watch In South India
item.description: Indian security agencies are closely monitoring signs of a possible resurgence of the Base Movement in South India, a group linked to Al-Qaeda. The outfit was active during 2015–16 and had carried out low-intensity blasts, largely targeting the judiciary and police.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: China sets up new county in Xinjiang near PoK, Afghan border
item.description: China has set up a new county in its volatile Xinjiang province near Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) and the Afghanistan border in an apparent move to beef up security along the narrow Wakhan Corridor to curb infiltration of Uyghur separatist militants.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India-UK free trade pact may come into force from second week of May: Official
item.description: The India-UK free trade agreement, signed in July last year, is likely to come into force from the second week of May, according to an official. India and the UK, on July 24, 2025, signed the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), under which 99 per cent of Indian exports will enter the British market at zero duty, while tariffs on British products, such as cars and whisky, will be reduced in India.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: As Naxalism ends in Chhattisgarh, village gets tap water for first time
item.description: The end of the Naxal menace in Chhattisgarh and with it the classification of Nelangur, a remote village in the state’s Narayanpur district, as a “highly sensitive” zone has finally resulted in residents there getting relief from their most worrisome problem – lack of water.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army, IAF Mark Siachen Day; Honour 42 Years Of ‘Operation Meghdoot’
item.description: The Indian Army and the Indian Air Force on Monday saluted the indomitable courage, unwavering commitment, and supreme sacrifice of India’s brave soldiers defending the world’s highest battlefield.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Last Senior Naxalite Neutralised In Chhattisgarh Encounter, Claims Police
item.description: In a major development, security forces neutralised a top female Naxalite leader in an encounter in Chhattisgarh’s Kanker district. The deceased has been identified as Rupi, an Area Committee Member (ACM) in the Naxalite hierarchy.
item.dc:creator: admin
